label: Dense Terminal
description: Developer-facing dashboard variant with terminal mood and dense operational UI.
signatureMotif: terminal chrome, dense panels, monospace hierarchy, and precise contrast
promptHints: Favor dense but legible information panels, tabs, and code-like framing over marketing-style spacing.
Use developer-friendly cues such as monospace, logs, traces, and command affordances where relevant.

layouts: Use a two-column operations shell with a narrow icon-and-label sidebar and a dense main pane stacked with tabs, metrics, and log tables.
Build the primary dashboard as a 12-column command center with compact stat cards on top, a wide trace or chart panel, and a right-side incident feed.
Compose detail pages with sticky subnav tabs, a left rail of resource metadata, and a dominant central console containing logs, code blocks, or traces.
Use split-pane screens where an interactive terminal or query editor sits left and live results, status badges, and event streams update on the right.
Structure settings and deployment pages as dense sectioned panels with inline descriptions, key-value grids, and footer action bars instead of airy form stacks.
motifs: Use terminal-style chrome with thin borders, muted dark surfaces, and header strips that feel like tabs or window bars.
Pair monospace headings, labels, and metrics with sans body copy so the hierarchy feels operational rather than editorial.
Favor compact status chips, command buttons, and key-value rows with precise contrast and restrained green or amber highlights.
Show code-like framing everywhere: log lines, trace timestamps, prompt blocks, and panel headers that resemble developer tooling.
antiPatterns: Avoid oversized marketing heroes, testimonial-style spacing, or full-bleed lifestyle imagery that weakens the operational terminal mood.
Never use soft pastel cards, glassmorphism blur, or colorful gradient backgrounds; this variant should stay sharp, dark, and utilitarian.
Avoid giant rounded tiles with sparse content; panels should feel information-dense, inspectable, and built for repeated scanning.
Do not center everything into a single-column narrative flow; this style depends on split panes, side rails, and concurrent information views.
